_comment: Asha declarative PreToolUse policy rules. Evaluated by hooks/handlers/policy-guard.sh. User overrides/additions: ~/.asha/policies.json (merged by id, user wins). Schema per rule: {id, tool (anchored ERE on tool_name), command_regex|file_path_regex (ERE matched against the Bash command / file path), action: deny|ask, reason, override_env?}. NOTE: this is a soft string-pattern DETERRENT, not containment — command_regex matches short -r/-R only (long flags like --recursive and indirection like 'cd /home && find .' slip through), and an agent can evade deliberately. Pair with the harness's own permission/sandbox controls for hard limits.

rules.reason: Broad scan over /home — /home is a slow spinning HDD with active Keybase I/O; broad find/grep/bfs/fd/rg cause severe disk pressure (Asha learning no-broad-home-scans, conf 0.95). Scope to a subdirectory, or set ASHA_ALLOW_BROAD_SCAN=1 to override.
